Hey guys and welcome to the workbook for module seven. This is the closing sequence and as you've just seen in the main lesson, an effective closing sequence can literally be the make or break of your launch. Up to 80% of your sales can come in these final hours. So it pays to pay some attention here and really integrate what you're learning and use the theme plates that we're going to be talking about in just a bit. And while surface level time based urgency from a countdown can be effective to bring in sales, the real opportunity to amplify your sales by an even bigger, greater degree is in countering a more insidious conversion killer, which is indecisiveness as well as the perception that a choice doesn't even need to be made, that the launch can just end in a whimper without them making a decision with just kind of that crappy little countdown, timer hitting zero, and then kind of wiping your hands clean like it never happened. So that is what we are trying to avoid here. We are instead trying to coach decisiveness and there are many tools we have at our disposal to do it. So what gets in the way of decision making sometimes are cognitive biases. So we can acknowledge and remove them by self forgiving past actions or inactions. If you have the six figure email pack, you will see market search for meaning, which is a great way to do this. Coaching decisiveness through exclusive empowerment, but this time to use and benefit from your product just like how we use that as the call out to get people into your funnel to begin with, we can use it on the back end to really make people feel exclusively empowered and privileged to use your product based on all those other sources we have discussed earlier in the course. We are also going to continue to facilitate the identification with being a buyer. We are going to introduce the concept of the minimum viable commitment. We spoke about this in the module before, but setting the bar just high enough that they could clear, but also not making it so that they need to be a thousand and 10% confident and get married today and live happily ever after. What's a minimum viable commitment for being in your program? We are going to support that with the quick, valuable win, which is something tangible that they can receive, that ROI they can receive within hours or days. If what you're doing doesn't have a monetary aspect to it, what is the million dollar pain that it solves? What's that quick, crunchy, valuable win they're going to be able to enjoy in the minutes and hours after joining a program? Of course, we're coaching binary choice, the fact that a choice does exist. We're going to say this pretty much literally in the sequence. There's no room to beat around the bush. We want them to know that a decision has to be made. They owe it to themselves to make a conscious choice, but we're going to enhance their ability to do it by giving them a sense of control, collaboration, and even co-creation in that final close. You'll see how that plays out in a few minutes. We're also going to be coaching, while we're coaching decisiveness through the use of second hand FOMO. Show our own fear of them missing out. Make the buying decision about more than just them. Their decision doesn't exist in the silo. It affects us and our mission, their family, their friends. They're stakeholders in their life. That second hand FOMO is a way to coach decisiveness. We will wrap this up with risk reversal, which combined with the risk revealer that we spoke about in the last module creates this dynamic of risk mitigation. Which step, which action, or inaction is going to be the less risky? That is what we're doing to coach decisiveness. It's time to explore some questions that are going to really help fuel the content for the theme plates we're about to write. You've answered some of these before and we've got some additional ones. These might sound familiar. Going back, what about your market struggles breaks your heart that you're going to use when you really channel that second hand FOMO? What reality do you see for them that they don't yet see for themselves? What pains you most about their inability to see it? Those are really going to be you invoking your own FOMO towards the end and making that decision more than just about them. Some additional questions. Complete the following sentence. You're in an exclusively empowered position to use my product and achieve promised outcome if. We've already done a lot of work on exclusive empowerment. I'm not going to dive into the mechanics of it now. There's another, there's lessons on it. What was it? Module 3, I believe. Go back to that if you need a primer on exclusive empowerment right now. What we're doing right now is using it within the frame of using your product and achieving that outcome. Then what's that minimum viable commitment? Is it tested risk brief for 30 days, put the lessons in module one into practice, go through the first audio, use it once, invest a dollar today? You just really want to lower that bar. It's not about you're joining forever and you've got to put 15 hours a week and be 120% committed. You really want to allow this to be inclusive of their doubts and skepticism about either you themselves or their product or all these beliefs you've reshifted. You've done a lot of work in creating those new beliefs, but maybe the paint hasn't been fully dried out yet. Maybe there's still some leakage. Maybe there's still some doubts spilling to the floor and you want to allow that to be okay. Allow that paint to dry after they've entered your program. So tell them it's all good if there's still a little bit concerned. Just simply go through the first audio, see if it's for you. That is the minimum viable commitment. I want you to decide what that looks like for your product. Next is the quickest and most valuable win that they'll achieve within hours or days of getting started. If you cover this, I'm not going to go into it more right now, but that's something you're going to want to be armed with by the time you start writing these theme plates. Then we're going to move into what's that ownership experience within minutes of your prospect enrolling? What will they see? What will they be doing? How can you prepave that reality to make it more familiar and more safe? What positive qualities or characteristics have they exhibited in the past that got them to this point? Can you reflect back those qualities to remind them of the power they have to decide and overcome resistance? You're going to see how this plays out in the Danny Tanner theme plate. So it's just giving them a chance to see that they have this ability in them. They've already demonstrated it in the past. Now it's not a matter of being someone they're not, but just channeling this better part of themselves that they're fully capable of. Do you have any stories of someone who got in the last minute and then became a breakthrough success story? So this is really good for continuing that identification with being a buyer aspect, especially if they could see that not all success stories are those supercharged action takers that got in on day one. Is there anyone who turned their life around by just sneaking in under the gate last second Indiana Jones style? Because that would be more inspiring and more likely for them to identify with at this stage of your launch. So that is what we're going to be working with. Those are the questions I want you to take a few minutes right now to explore and write your answers because it's going to serve us a lot when it comes to writing the theme plates coming up. So take a few minutes to do that and I'll meet you soon in the closing theme plates.
